channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PCSO reports no jackpot winners; Superlotto 6/49 prize rises to ₱164 million
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that the Superlotto 6/49 jackpot has grown to more than ₱164 million after Sunday night’s draw on April 5, 2026, with no ticket matching all ...

item.title: Van Dijk accepts blame as Liverpool suffer 4-0 defeat to Manchester City in FA Cup
item.description: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk don come out to apologise to the club’s supporters after dem chop 4-0 beating for the FA Cup quarter-final against Manchester City. The match wey happen for Etihad St...

item.title: Community Mural Project Lets Artists Contribute to a San Francisco Alley
item.description: In San Francisco, a narrow 82-foot alley became the center of an innovative art project after a couple accidentally overpaid for the land. Tech enthusiasts Patrick Hultquist, Theo Bleier, and Riley Wa...

item.title: Odisha revises education reservation: SC, ST quotas increased; OBC quota introduced
item.description: The Odisha government has significantly revised its reservation policy in medical and technical education. At a cabinet meeting led by Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi, it was decided to raise the qu...

item.title: Zelensky fears Middle East war may limit US military aid to Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has voiced deep concern that a drawn-out war involving the United States, Israel, and Iran could undermine Washington’s support for Ukraine as its resources and ...

item.title: Review: Paul Feig’s ‘The Housemaid’ blends suspense and female rivalry with uneven results
item.description: Paul Feig’s film adaptation of Freida McFadden’s bestselling thriller, *The Housemaid*, is a glossy yet dark drama that explores deceit, manipulation and gendered power struggles. The story follows Mi...

item.title: Mt. Pulag Trails Temporarily Closed Due to Forest Fire
item.description: Authorities have temporarily closed all trails on Mt. Pulag, a popular destination for hikers and nature enthusiasts, due to a forest fire that broke out recently. The fire, the cause of which is stil...

item.title: Leeds defeat West Ham on penalties to reach FA Cup semifinals
item.description: Leeds United secured their place in the FA Cup semifinals after a dramatic quarterfinal clash against West Ham United that ended 2-2 after extra time and was decided by penalties. The match kicked off...

item.title: Rotary Club trains Kinondoni teachers on inclusive teaching methods
item.description: The Rotary Club of Dar es Salaam has once again shown commitment to improving education quality by organizing a two-day training seminar for primary school teachers in Kinondoni Municipality. This tra...

item.title: Julie Gascon steps down as CEO of Montreal Port Authority amid ongoing expansion efforts
item.description: Julie Gascon has stepped down from her position as president and CEO of the Montreal Port Authority, ending a tenure of less than two years. The announcement was made on Friday through a brief stateme...

item.title: Former CIA Chief Explains Deception Strategy in Iran Airman Rescue
item.description: Former CIA station chief Dan Hoffman detailed a sophisticated intelligence operation that assisted in the rescue of a U.S. Air Force Weapons System Officer (WSO) after his F-15E fighter jet went down ...

item.title: Neuroimaging study shows how voluntary trance states reorganize brain connectivity without drugs
item.description: A new neuroimaging case study published in the journal NeuroImage explored how a woman can voluntarily enter a transcendental visionary state that closely resembles psychedelic experiences but without...

item.title: Delhi Police Bust International Cyber Fraud Syndicate Involving Rs 300 Crore
item.description: In a significant law enforcement operation, Delhi Police have successfully dismantled an international cyber fraud syndicate responsible for defrauding people of nearly Rs 300 crore. The investigation...

item.title: Teenager arrested on suspicion of rape after woman attacked in Lowestoft alleyway
item.description: An 18-year-old man has been arrested following the alleged rape of a woman in her twenties in Lowestoft, a seaside town in Suffolk. The incident reportedly occurred shortly after midnight when the wom...

item.title: Archaeologists Uncover Roman Sanctuary in Frankfurt Revealing Ritual Practices
item.description: A recently uncovered Roman sanctuary in Frankfurt, Germany, is providing unprecedented insights into ancient religious practices. Excavations at the site of Nida, the Roman-era city in Frankfurt-Hedde...

item.title: Understanding the Origins of Christian Beliefs on Jesus’ Resurrection
item.description: As Easter draws near, Christians across the globe focus on the death and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth, a key pillar of their faith. While several Jewish teachers and miracle workers were active i...

item.title: Mandela family internal dispute over occupation and control of Nelson Mandela's Houghton residence heads to court
item.description: A long-standing family disagreement within the Mandela family has intensified regarding the occupation of the late Nelson Mandela's home in Houghton, Johannesburg. Tukwini Mandela, granddaughter of th...

item.title: Fatuma Zarika to make boxing return in Europe after break
item.description: Kenya’s celebrated boxer Fatuma Zarika, known for blazing the trail in women’s boxing, has revealed her plans to mount a comeback in Europe after some time away from the ring. The former World Boxing ...

item.title: Gold price volatility challenges its status as a safe investment during crises
item.description: Gold has traditionally been viewed as a safe haven during financial turbulence, but recent months have shown that this perception may no longer hold true. The price of gold surged to a record high of ...

item.title: UCLA Wins First NCAA Women's Basketball Championship With Decisive Victory Over South Carolina
item.description: UCLA captured its first NCAA women's basketball championship with a commanding 79-51 victory over South Carolina on April 5, 2026. Gabriela Jaquez led the Bruins with 21 points, adding 10 rebounds and...
